scope: Scope of Car Models Analyzed: 134 trims were analyzed across six years & 13 car models.
count_note: Notes: The Toyota Highlander EV and Tesla Model Y Long (Asia) are not yet available in the US, but were included for reference. Rivian R1S Gen1 (2022–2024) and Gen2 (2025+) models are both included.

details.name: Kia EV9, 2026, GT-Line
details.self_driving: Kia Highway Driving Assist 2 / Lane Following Assist (ADAS)
details.car_software: Android Auto + Apple CarPlay; ccNC infotainment
details.main_display: 12.3" display unit w/ nav + 12.3" cluster + 5" HVAC display
details.additional_displays: 12.3" cluster + 5" HVAC display; Full Display Mirror available on higher trims
details.audio: Meridian Premium Audio (14 speakers) on Land/GT-Line; 8-speaker on lower trims

glossary.notes: Used as a compact grouping label, such as “Tesla Model X” or “Kia EV9.”
Usually the broader vehicle family name used for grouping rows across years/trims.
Example: 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026. This is the manufacturer’s model year, not the calendar year of sale.
Identifies the exact version being compared, including battery, drivetrain, or seating differences where relevant.
Total number of passengers the vehicle is configured to carry, such as 6 or 7.
Example: RWD, AWD, Dual Motor AWD, etc.
Base sticker price before destination, taxes, dealer fees, incentives, or markups.
Standard manufacturer freight/delivery fee added to MSRP on new vehicles.
Estimated used-market listing price range from pricing aggregators such as Kelley Blue Book and Edmunds.

glossary.meaning: Plain-English summary of driver assistance / autonomy capability
glossary.notes: General description of the vehicle’s most advanced available hands-free / supervised driving system.

glossary.meaning: EPA-rated or estimated driving range in miles
glossary.notes: Uses EPA range where available; otherwise estimated / reported range if EPA data is not yet available.
Manufacturer-reported or widely cited total system horsepower.
Usually gross or commonly cited pack capacity; may be approximate depending on what manufacturers disclose.
Shows the car’s AC charging hardware capability, which affects home / Level 2 charging speed.

glossary.meaning: Estimated Level 2 charging time from 10% to 80% in number of hours
glossary.notes: Derived estimate for the more practical 10%–80% AC charging window.
Example: NACS or CCS. Reflects the vehicle’s native port, not just adapter compatibility.
Notes whether the vehicle supports Apple CarPlay / Android Auto, and if not, what native infotainment ecosystem it uses instead.

glossary.meaning: Size of the primary center display
glossary.notes: Main infotainment screen size, typically in inches.

glossary.meaning: Other displays beyond the main screen
glossary.notes: Includes digital gauge cluster, passenger screen, rear screen, head-up display (HUD), or other secondary displays.
Describes the vehicle’s sound system branding or speaker setup, where known.

glossary.meaning: Whether the vehicle supports distinct saved driver settings
glossary.notes: Indicates whether different drivers can save personalized settings such as seat, mirrors, steering, climate, and software preferences.

glossary.meaning: The cargo capacity of the front trunk (frunk), measured in cubic feet.
glossary.notes: A storage compartment under the front hood, made possible by the absence of a combustion engine. Size varies widely — from minimal (1–2 cu ft) to large enough for a carry-on suitcase (11+ cu ft).

glossary.meaning: The trunk/cargo volume behind the third row of seats when all seats are upright, measured in cubic feet.
glossary.notes: This is the everyday usable trunk space with all rows occupied. Typically the smallest cargo measurement since it only counts the area behind the rearmost seats.

glossary.meaning: The cargo volume behind the second row of seats when the third row is folded down or removed, measured in cubic feet.
glossary.notes: A substantially larger cargo area created by folding the 3rd row flat. Useful for bulky items like strollers, luggage, or furniture. Vehicles without a 3rd row (5-seat configurations) may not have this measurement.

glossary.meaning: The maximum cargo volume with all rear seats folded, measured from behind the front seats to the tailgate, in cubic feet.
glossary.notes: The largest possible cargo configuration. Useful for moving furniture, large appliances, or maximizing hauling capacity. This completes the cargo measurement trio: behind 3rd row (trunk), behind 2nd row (3rd folded), behind 1st row (everything folded).

glossary.meaning: Whether the rear seat rows fold completely flat to create a level cargo floor.
glossary.notes: Values: Yes (seats fold fully flat), No (seats do not fold flat, leaving a stepped surface), or Partial (some rows fold flat but not all, e.g. 2nd-row captain's chairs may not fold). A flat cargo floor makes it easier to slide in large or heavy items.

glossary.meaning: The width of the cargo floor between the interior wheel wells, measured in inches.
glossary.notes: This is the narrowest point of the cargo area and determines the widest items you can lay flat. Important for fitting wide objects like sheets of plywood, large boxes, or a mattress.

glossary.meaning: Source links used for that row
glossary.notes: URL list or source references used to build the row’s data. Useful for validation and traceability.
Used for things like “not yet released,” “market data thin,” “seat package optional,” or other row-specific caveats.

assumptions.notes: PA base sales tax (Delaware County is 6%)
Typical PA doc fee estimate; varies by dealer
PA annual fee estimate for EVs; check current schedule
